_cite.reason: 4c: relabel unlabeled workspace-analysis to labeled analysis (782878c0 §14); every binding sentence judged WORKSPACE_ANALYSIS

prior.content_md:
| Date | Milestone | What the workspace should have done by this date |
|---|---|---|
| **1 January 2025** | Order in force. Main obligation at 2.041%. Suppliers exit RTFO eligibility for SAF. | Carrier SAF cost impact assessment begun. Client briefing materials in development. Legal review of workspace role initiated. |
| **12 May 2025 (annual)** | SAF certificate application deadline for suppliers (for preceding obligation period). | No direct action required by workspace. Confirm with carrier partners that their certificates are applied for on schedule — delays may affect data availability. |
| **15 September 2025 (annual)** | Supplier notification deadline: number of SAF certificates to count toward obligation discharge. Buy-out payment period opens. | No direct action required by workspace. Carrier compliance status may affect pricing and supply continuity. |
| **26 October 2025 (annual)** | Buy-out payment deadline: suppliers must pay buy-out amount for any unfulfilled obligation from preceding calendar year. | If carriers resort to buy-out rather than physical SAF supply, this indicates supply shortage conditions — flag to commercial team as potential precursor to surcharge increase. |
| **1 January 2026** | Obligation increases to 3.734% (main). | Rate review with carrier partners. |
| **1 January 2027** | Obligation increases to 5.485% (main). **HEFA cap begins at 4.80% of total jet fuel** (92.31% of main obligation). This is the first year when non-HEFA SAF must begin filling the gap. | Assess carrier non-HEFA SAF procurement plans. Begin modelling 2027–2028 cost trajectory for UK air cargo clients. |
| **1 January 2028** | Obligation: 7.082% main + 0.215% PtL. **PtL obligation begins.** | Confirm PtL SAF certificate availability with carrier partners. Update client emissions reporting methodology to distinguish PtL from HEFA SAF. |
| **By end 2026 (expected)** | Revenue Certainty Mechanism (RCM) expected to be implemented by UK government. | Monitor for RCM contract awards to non-HEFA SAF producers. First tranche of RCM contracts for non-HEFA production only. |
| **Before 1 January 2030** | First statutory five-year review of the Order published. Potential changes to GHG thresholds, targets, and HEFA cap reviewed. | Monitor review findings. Update client guidance as necessary. |
| **1 January 2030** | Obligation: 10.556% main + 0.556% PtL. HEFA cap: 7.10% of total jet fuel. | SAF now ~10% of UK jet fuel. Cost premium likely moderated by RCM if implemented. Full reassessment of carrier cost structures recommended. |
| **1 January 2040 (and subsequent periods)** | Obligation: 23.718% main + 4.487% PtL. HEFA cap: 7.80% of total jet fuel (and subsequent periods). | Long-range planning horizon; RCM, HEFA cap, and PtL sub-mandate will have materially reshaped UK SAF market. |

---
